title: Travel Trailer and Camper Tax Parity Act
short_title: Travel Trailer and Camper Tax Parity Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
plain_summary: This bill changes tax rules for businesses that sell travel trailers and campers. Right now, businesses can get special tax breaks when they borrow money to buy cars and trucks to sell in their stores. But they cannot get the same tax breaks when they borrow money to buy travel trailers and campers for their stores. This bill would let businesses that sell travel trailers and campers get the same tax benefits as businesses that sell regular cars and trucks.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Travel Trailer and Camper Tax Parity Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Floor Plan Financing Applicable to Certain Trailers and Campers
sections.text: This section changes the tax rules about floor plan financing. Floor plan financing is a special type of loan that businesses use to buy inventory they plan to sell. The tax code currently limits how much businesses can deduct for interest on most loans, but it makes an exception for floor plan financing interest. This law expands that exception to include trailers and campers. Specifically, it includes any trailer or camper that is designed to provide temporary living space for recreation, camping, or seasonal use and is designed to be pulled by a motor vehicle or attached to one. These changes will apply to tax years that begin after December thirty first, twenty twenty four.
